channel.title: ainews — Karl's daily AI news
channel.description: Daily AI news, deduped + tagged for hotloop's workflows

item.title: Brockman: Codex workspace agents use cloud-hosted harness with scoped tools and tasks
item.description: OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman describes Codex workspace agents running on a cloud-hosted harness with tools and tasks scoped per session. Practical architecture signal for teams building production agentic coding systems on top of Codex.

item.title: Gemma 4 VLA Demo on Jetson Orin Nano Super
item.description: NVIDIA demonstrates Google's Gemma 4 Vision-Language-Action model on the Jetson Orin Nano Super for robotics. The demo shows Gemma 4's VLA capabilities enabling real-time robot control from natural language instructions on edge hardware.

item.title: NVIDIA and Google Cloud Collaborate to Advance Agentic and Physical AI
item.description: NVIDIA and Google Cloud deepen a decade-long collaboration into a full-stack AI platform targeting agentic and physical AI. The partnership focuses on production deployment of autonomous agents and AI factories, with joint engineering across NVIDIA's compute stack and Google Cloud's managed services.

item.title: Google launches two specialized 8th-gen TPUs for the agentic era
item.description: Google unveiled the 8th-generation TPU with two specialised chips — v8t for training and v8i for inference — purpose-built for agentic AI workloads powering Gemini. Major performance gains over prior generations confirmed at Google Cloud Next.

item.title: Chollet: judging AGI by mimicry is a category error — measure by learning what wasn't taught
item.description: François Chollet argues that evaluating AGI by how well systems mimic human outputs is a category error. True AGI should be measured by how well a system learns to do things it was not explicitly trained on, including tasks humans themselves cannot perform.

item.title: Speeding up agentic workflows with WebSockets in the Responses API
item.description: OpenAI details how WebSocket connections and connection-scoped caching in the Responses API reduced latency in the Codex agent loop. Persistent connections cut API overhead in long-running agent tasks, with benchmarks showing the improvement.

item.title: Introducing OpenAI Privacy Filter
item.description: OpenAI releases Privacy Filter, an open-weight model for detecting and redacting PII in text with claimed state-of-the-art accuracy. Available as a standalone model for developers building privacy-compliant pipelines without closed API dependencies.

item.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
item.description: NVIDIA walks through running Google Gemma 4 locally on RTX PCs and DGX Sparks for agentic tasks without cloud dependency. Practical guide for developers deploying Gemma 4 at the edge for latency-sensitive agent applications.

item.title: The Future of AI Is Open and Proprietary
item.description: NVIDIA argues the AI future isn't a binary open-vs-closed debate: proprietary models drive frontier capabilities while open models enable customisation and local deployment. Maps which approach wins in each enterprise scenario.

item.title: How Autonomous AI Agents Become Secure by Design With NVIDIA OpenShell
item.description: NVIDIA's OpenShell runtime provides a secure execution environment for autonomous agents, enforcing least-privilege policies and sandboxing agent actions. Relevant for anyone deploying production agents that interact with external systems or sensitive data.

item.title: Grok 4.1 Fast and Agent Tools API
item.description: xAI releases Grok 4.1 Fast, a speed-optimised variant of Grok 4, alongside an Agent Tools API for building agentic workflows. Positions Grok as a low-latency inference option for agent applications and opens programmatic tool use to developers.

item.title: xAI partners with US Department of War on AI for national security
item.description: xAI announced a partnership with the US Department of War to deploy Grok models for national security applications. Extends the pattern of frontier AI labs securing large government defence contracts alongside commercial enterprise expansion.

item.title: xAI and El Salvador launch world's first nationwide AI education programme
item.description: xAI and El Salvador's government launched what they describe as the world's first nationwide AI education programme, integrating Grok-powered tools into the school system. Part of xAI's broader international government partnership strategy.

item.title: Grok goes global with Saudi Arabia partnership
item.description: xAI announced a partnership with Saudi Arabia to expand Grok's global footprint, part of a broader international push. Adds KSA to xAI's growing list of government and regional AI deployment partnerships.

item.title: Expanding xAI for Government with GSA OneGov
item.description: xAI expands its government offering via a GSA OneGov contract, making Grok accessible to US federal agencies through standard government procurement. Signals a serious enterprise-government market entry with structured acquisition pathway.

item.title: SAM 3.1: Faster Real-Time Video Detection and Tracking With Multiplexing
item.description: Meta releases SAM 3.1, an improved Segment Anything Model with multiplexing for higher throughput and global reasoning for better tracking across complex video scenes. Faster and more accessible than SAM 2 for real-time video AI applications.

item.title: Scaling How We Build and Test Our Most Advanced AI
item.description: Meta details the engineering pipeline for building and evaluating its most advanced AI systems at scale, covering infrastructure, evaluation frameworks, and red-teaming. Provides insight into Meta's frontier model development practices.

item.title: Four MTIA Chips in Two Years: Scaling AI Experiences for Billions
item.description: Meta outlines four generations of MTIA chips in two years, built to power AI experiences for billions of users. Custom silicon strategy reduces inference costs versus GPU-only approaches at consumer scale.

item.title: Demand for AI-related skills is growing and older NZ workers are acing the pivot
item.description: RNZ reports demand for AI-related skills in New Zealand has grown sharply in 2026, with 300,000 new AI skill sets projected. Surveys show older workers are successfully retraining, challenging the assumption that AI upskilling skews young.

item.title: Where's the raccoon with the ham radio? (ChatGPT Images 2.0)
item.description: Simon Willison tested ChatGPT Images 2.0 with a Where's-Waldo-style prompt and compares output against gpt-image-1. Sam Altman claimed on the livestream that the leap was equivalent to jumping from GPT-3 to GPT-5; Simon's review adds nuance from real prompts.

item.title: Introducing ChatGPT Images 2.0 with Instant and Thinking variants
item.description: OpenAI released ChatGPT Images 2.0 globally with Instant and Thinking variants that can generate up to 2K-resolution images, render non-Latin scripts (Japanese, Korean, Hindi, Bengali) more accurately, and optionally search the web while composing an image. A more powerful tier is reserved for paying subscribers.

item.title: Three new ways Ads Advisor is making Google Ads safer and faster
item.description: Google announced three new agentic safety and policy features integrated into Ads Advisor, designed to protect advertiser accounts and streamline campaign creation. The update adds real-time policy checks and automated anomaly detection for generated ads.

item.title: Chollet: current AI systems lack metacognition — they cannot accurately model their own capabilities
item.description: François Chollet argues that LLMs fundamentally lack metacognition: they cannot accurately assess what they know or don't know, producing confident errors and unreliable self-evaluation. He frames this as a key gap separating current systems from robust general reasoning, with direct implications for building reliable agentic pipelines.

item.title: QIMMA: A Quality-First Arabic LLM Leaderboard
item.description: Hugging Face and TII launch QIMMA, a quality-first leaderboard for Arabic LLMs covering dialect, culture, and reasoning benchmarks. Fills a critical gap in non-English model evaluation, relevant for any multilingual or regional AI deployment work.

item.title: LeCun: AI existential risk framing is wrong — misuse and power concentration are the real dangers
item.description: Yann LeCun pushes back on p(doom) framing, arguing the real AI risk is not autonomous superintelligence but concentration of AI power in a few companies and misuse by state or corporate actors. He calls for open-source AI development and international governance structures as the appropriate countermeasures.

item.title: OpenAI Images v2 rolling out to all ChatGPT accounts
item.description: OpenAI Images v2, an upgraded image generation capability, is now available across all ChatGPT tiers. Confirmed via Logan Kilpatrick on X.

item.title: Anthropic launches Claude Design, hiring senior designers at $400K+
item.description: Anthropic announces Claude Design and is hiring senior designers at $400K+ compensation. Signals a design-first product direction for Claude alongside the engineering-centric Claude Code push.

item.title: When AI quietly shifts what organisations value
item.description: Tech New Zealand examines how generative AI embedded in education practice can gradually reshape institutional values — a "value drift" similar to how social media quietly rewrote norms around privacy. Relevant for Aotearoa organisations adopting AI without explicit policy guardrails.

item.title: Anthropic says OpenClaw-style Claude CLI usage is allowed again
item.description: Anthropic has updated its provider documentation to permit OpenClaw-style Claude CLI integrations after a prior restriction. The move re-opens a popular path for local agent tooling that had been in limbo for two weeks. HN front page with 465 points.

item.title: Google: Gemini expands in Chrome to more Asia-Pacific countries with summarization and image editing
item.description: Google announced Gemini features in Chrome are expanding to more Asia-Pacific countries, adding page summarization, image editing, and broader integrations. The rollout extends Google's browser-native AI layer to a wider international audience as part of Chrome's AI-first roadmap.
